title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, say why numbers might be important in these situations and how difficult it might be to make sure the numbers are correct.

images.label: A
images.description: A close-up of a runner's wrist showing a digital sports watch with a timer and split-second numbers during a race.

images.label: B
images.description: A stock market trader's desk with multiple computer monitors displaying fluctuating red and green numbers and complex financial charts.

images.label: C
images.description: A mathematician or scientist standing at a chalkboard covered in long, complex numerical equations and formulas.

followUp.question: In which situation do you think it is most likely that mistakes might be made? (Why?)

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, say how important it might be for the people to use wood in these situations and how enjoyable their experience might be.

images.label: D
images.description: A skilled woodworker in a workshop using a chisel to carefully carve a decorative pattern into a piece of light-colored wood.

images.label: E
images.description: A person outdoors in winter, using a heavy axe to split large logs of wood for use as fuel in a fireplace.

images.label: F
images.description: Construction workers standing on a high wooden scaffolding, assembling the large timber frame of a new house.

followUp.question: Who do you think will remember their experience the longest? (Why?)
